label: Weekly review
brief: You are my marketing analyst. Last week’s metrics: [PASTE NUMBERS OR SCREENSHOT SUMMARY]. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Channels: [CHANNELS]. Give me: (1) the single biggest insight from the data, (2) three prioritized actions for next week with owner + deadline, (3) one metric to watch and why.
nextAction: Schedule a 20-minute metrics review with the owner named for action #1.
bottomLine: Three actions beat ten vague ideas—ship the smallest test that reduces uncertainty.
risks: Cherry-picking one spike without checking sample size
Optimizing vanity metrics (likes) instead of pipeline or revenue signals
questions: Which number changed week-over-week and by how much?
What did we intentionally not do last week that could explain the result?

label: Campaign launch brief
brief: You are my campaign lead. Objective: [ONE SENTENCE GOAL]. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Offer: [OFFER]. Channels: [CHANNELS]. Budget/time box: [CONSTRAINT]. Produce: (1) core message in one line, (2) three hook variants aligned to the objective, (3) channel-specific tweaks (max 2 bullets per channel), (4) KPIs with targets and measurement window.
nextAction: Pick one hook + one channel for a 7-day pilot before scaling spend.
bottomLine: One objective, one primary KPI—everything else is diagnostic.
risks: Hooks that promise outcomes we cannot substantiate
Too many KPIs so nobody owns the primary metric
questions: What would make us stop or pivot this campaign?
What proof do we need before we scale creative or budget?

label: Stakeholder update
brief: You are my communications partner. I need a stakeholder update for [AUDIENCE / ROLE]. Decision needed: [YES/NO OR CHOICE]. Context: [2–4 bullets]. Risks: [BULLETS]. Produce: (1) bottom line in 2 sentences, (2) recommendation with trade-offs, (3) explicit ask (decision, budget, or timeline), (4) next meeting or follow-up if no reply.
nextAction: Send the draft and put the decision deadline in the subject line.
bottomLine: If the decision isn’t obvious in 30 seconds, rewrite the top.
risks: Soft language that avoids ownership or dates
questions: What happens if we do nothing for two weeks?
Who can approve this on their own authority?
